demographic_audit_note: Verified Medicare Advantage eligibility; found address mismatch between Atlantis and facility face sheet (Zip code 19104 vs 19143). Corrected primary phone to daughter's mobile for POA access.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker confirms the goal is Home with HHA services for PT and nursing oversight following MS steroid taper.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient completed IV steroids. SW reports gait is stabilizing. Target discharge set for Friday; however, HHA reported a temporary intake department freeze due to a regional system outage.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card successfully saved to patient's smartphone during bedside education session; physical program flyer delivered to daughter/POA.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached POA 24 hours prior to discharge. Confirmed hospital bed and O2 are in place. Alerted family that the HHA nurse visit is delayed due to the intake freeze, but our MA visit remains scheduled.
atlantis_final_sync: Discharge date entered as Friday, 10/27. First visit (MA) scheduled for Saturday, 10/28. Logged in Atlantis queue.
ma_visit_booking: MA scheduled for Saturday at 11:00 AM to ensure the 24-hour post-discharge window is met despite the HHA intake backlog.
narrative_summary: The patient, a 68yo female recovering from an MS flare, was tracked from intake through a complex Friday discharge. During Stage 2, I identified a regional HHA Intake Freeze that threatened the 'First Visit' window, necessitating intensive family education on the transition timeline. In Stage 3, I successfully bypassed the HHA's operational delay by scheduling our Medical Assistant for a Saturday morning visit, securing the transition incentive and ensuring the patient was not left unmonitored over the weekend.

format_1_state_log.event_description: HHA_Intake_Freeze: System outage at regional agency prevents new admission processing.
Friday Discharge Gap: Facility proceeds with discharge despite HHA inability to staff a weekend RN.
MA Scheduling Bridge: PN secures Saturday morning slot for Medical Assistant assessment.

format_2_triples.situation: HHA intake coordinator reports a freeze on new referrals due to a system-wide outage.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Flagged the intake delay to the Social Worker and requested a status update on the referral's internal 'Hub' receipt.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient's daughter expresses fear about managing MS symptoms over the weekend without a nurse.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated the daughter on the MA's role in verifying home safety and medication setup while the HHA nurse visit is pending.

format_2_triples.situation: Atlantis demographics list an outdated landline for the patient.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Audited the facility face sheet and updated Atlantis with the correct POA mobile number to ensure pulse call success.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Social Worker to identify an alternative HHA that is not currently under an intake freeze.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW's boundary as the sole party responsible for agency selection and referral management.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Schedule the Medical Assistant for a Saturday morning visit and hand the daughter a physical appointment card to bridge the HHA's weekend staffing gap.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Follows the Stage 3 checklist to ensure a 24-hour visit occurs, maintaining the transition incentive within scope.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call the HHA's intake director directly to advocate for the patient's referral and offer to help complete the Face-to-Face (F2F) paperwork.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Violates the SW boundary by interfering with HHA operations and the 'Banned Actions' regarding F2F forms.
